entry.text: A cairn of flat stones sits off the trail, deliberately built — three stones stacked vertically on top mark it as a cache, not a grave. Scratched into the top stone: a name and an arrow pointing down. The surrounding scrub is undisturbed but the soil beneath the cairn is darker, looser.

choices.label: Dismantle the cairn and dig carefully
Check the perimeter for the owner first

cache.text: Two feet down, a waterproof box the size of a bread loaf. Inside: food, a small jar of purification tablets, and a handwritten note listing three more cache locations — all in a cipher you can't read. You pocket the note anyway. The cipher might be worth something to the right trader.

choices.label: Dig deeper — the box felt too light
Backtrack and check the marker stone's base

secondary.text: Below the box, wrapped in oilskin: a compact medkit, two scrap-bound fuel cells, and a sealant tube marked with military stencilling. Whoever {{name}} was, they cached against a very specific emergency. You take it all and restack the cairn out of habit — some courtesies survive the apocalypse.

trap_wire.text: A trip-wire at ankle height snaps taut and releases a spring-loaded noise maker — a tin of stones that clatters across the scrub for thirty metres. Your heart hammers. No one comes, but they might.

ambush.text: The owner is still here — gaunt, desperate, and armed with a length of rebar. You take a blow across the shoulder before managing to talk them down and agree to split the find.

false_bottom.text: The false bottom is rigged to a pressure plate that fires a spring-loaded spike into your forearm as you lift it. The spike is short but the puncture is deep.

scorpion_nest.text: You upend the marker stone onto a colony of pale scorpions. Two land on your wrist before you can shake them loose, and the stings are already swelling.
